title: Gemini Erasure: Entropy-Reset Mechanism for Conformal Cyclic Cosmology combining conformal rescaling and work extraction
author: Watt, Henry
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Abstract 
 Each cosmological aeon begins in a state of strikingly low gravitational entropy. Matter is hot and dense but smoothly distributed; gravitational degrees of freedom carry almost no entropy; the Weyl curvature is essentially zero. This low-entropy initial condition (sometimes called the Past Hypothesis) is what gives the universe its arrow of time, but it has never been derived — it is imposed as a separate assumption, both in standard cosmology and in Penrose's Conformal Cyclic Cosmology (CCC) where it is stated as the Weyl Curvature Hypothesis. 
 We propose a mechanism. The aeon-to-aeon transition is understood as a composite operation combining two ingredients: Penrose's conformal rescaling at future conformal infinity, which takes the dilute massless content at the end of one aeon to the hot dense content at the start of the next; and a del Rio-style work extraction protocol, which acts on the end-of-aeon bulk-boundary entanglement and, by consuming those correlations, leaves the post-extraction state with zero conditional entropy between the new bulk and its complement. Under a holographic identification of conditional entanglement entropy with gravitational entropy, this entropy reset is identified with the low-Weyl-curvature condition of each new aeon's Big Bang. 
 The composite operation produces both halves of each aeon's initial condition — hot matter-radiation content and low gravitational entropy — from a single physical transition. The low-entropy Big Bang becomes a consequence of the entropy reset performed by extraction, not an independent postulate. The derivation rests on one unrigorous link (the holographic identification) that this document states explicitly and identifies as the key target for further work.
steps_raw:
Abstract 
 Each cosmological aeon begins in a state of strikingly low gravitational entropy. Matter is hot and dense but smoothly distributed; gravitational degrees of freedom carry almost no entropy; the Weyl curvature is essentially zero. This low-entropy initial condition (sometimes called the Past Hypothesis) is what gives the universe its arrow of time, but it has never been derived — it is imposed as a separate assumption, both in standard cosmology and in Penrose's Conformal Cyclic Cosmology (CCC) where it is stated as the Weyl Curvature Hypothesis. 
 We propose a mechanism. The aeon-to-aeon transition is understood as a composite operation combining two ingredients: Penrose's conformal rescaling at future conformal infinity, which takes the dilute massless content at the end of one aeon to the hot dense content at the start of the next; and a del Rio-style work extraction protocol, which acts on the end-of-aeon bulk-boundary entanglement and, by consuming those correlations, leaves the post-extraction state with zero conditional entropy between the new bulk and its complement. Under a holographic identification of conditional entanglement entropy with gravitational entropy, this entropy reset is identified with the low-Weyl-curvature condition of each new aeon's Big Bang. 
 The composite operation produces both halves of each aeon's initial condition — hot matter-radiation content and low gravitational entropy — from a single physical transition. The low-entropy Big Bang becomes a consequence of the entropy reset performed by extraction, not an independent postulate.
